data.content:
The file is generated successfully but never added to the compilation. The `GenerateSampleCode` target writes the `.cs` file but doesn't include it in the `Compile` item group, so Roslyn never sees it.

**Fix:** Add the generated file to `Compile` inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. By the time the file is generated, MSBuild has already collected the list of files to compile (the `Compile` items are evaluated before the target runs).

The fix is to add the generated file to the `Compile` item group within the target, using an `<ItemGroup>` inside the target:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />


</ItemGroup>
```

Adding this after the file is written ensures the compiler will pick it up.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly now.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote `GeneratedInfo.cs` to disk but never added it to the `Compile` item group. MSBuild collects items before targets run, so the dynamically created file was invisible to the compiler.

**Fix:** Added an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="..." /></ItemGroup>` inside the target so the generated file is included in compilation.
